cases.title: The Equator (0°)
cases.description: The Equator divides the world into Northern and Southern Hemispheres.
steps: Definition: Latitude is the distance North or South of the Equator in degrees.
PLE Fact: In Africa, it crosses 7 countries: Gabon, Congo, DRC, Uganda, Kenya, Somalia, and Sao Tome.
Climate: Places on this line experience the Equinox (Sun overhead) on March 21st and September 23rd.

cases.title: Tropic of Cancer (23½°N)
cases.description: The Northern limit of the overhead sun.
steps: This line is located 23½° North of the Equator.
It passes through North African countries: Egypt, Libya, Algeria, Mali, Mauritania, Western Sahara.
Solstice: The sun is directly overhead here on June 21st.
Areas North of this line never see the sun directly overhead.

cases.title: Tropic of Capricorn (23½°S)
cases.description: The Southern limit of the overhead sun.
steps: This line is located 23½° South of the Equator.
It passes through: Namibia, Botswana, South Africa, Mozambique, Madagascar.
Solstice: The sun is directly overhead here on December 22nd.
Fact: Africa is the only continent crossed by all 3 major latitudes (Equator, Cancer, Capricorn).

cases.title: The Torrid (Tropical) Zone
cases.description: Why Africa is the hottest continent.
steps: The orange shaded area is the Torrid Zone (Tropical Zone).
This area receives Direct Sunlight throughout the year.
Since 75% of Africa lies in this zone, it is the hottest continent.
